top_stories.title: Chinese hackers hijack auth flow, spy on isolated network for a decade
top_stories.summary: Chinese hackers compromised a target organization's authentication infrastructure and maintained persistent access for approximately 10 years. The attackers hijacked the authentication flow to gain full visibility into administrative activity on an isolated network.

top_stories.title: Securing AI agents in Microsoft Entra with Conditional Access
top_stories.summary: Microsoft is updating its Microsoft Entra identity security framework to address risks posed by autonomous AI agents. The update involves applying Conditional Access policies to AI agents, as traditional MFA is insufficient for non-human entities operating at machine speed and scale.

top_stories.title: CISA orders feds to patch actively exploited Ivanti flaw by Sunday
top_stories.summary: CISA issued Binding Operational Directive (BOD) 26-04 ordering federal agencies to patch an actively exploited Ivanti Sentry vulnerability within three days. Ivanti Sentry is an access management gateway product used to secure mobile device access to enterprise systems, making this directly relevant to IAM infrastructure security.

top_stories.title: China-Linked Hackers Backdoored Linux Login Software to Hide for Nearly a Decade
top_stories.summary: A China-linked threat group tracked as Velvet Ant backdoored Linux PAM (Pluggable Authentication Modules) and OpenSSH components to maintain persistent covert access for nearly a decade. The attack targeted the authentication layer itself, meaning standard incident response cleanup efforts would fail to detect or remove the implanted access.

top_stories.title: ServiceNow Flaw Exploited to Gain Unauthorized Access to Customer Instances
top_stories.summary: ServiceNow disclosed a security incident in which threat actors exploited a vulnerability to gain unauthorized access to customer instances, with a patch applied on June 5, 2026. The flaw allowed unauthenticated users to obtain deeper access to susceptible hosted instances, raising concerns for organizations using ServiceNow for identity workflows and access management.

news.title: Ex-school district employee jailed for hacks on former employer
news.summary: A former IT employee at an Iowa school district was sentenced to 21 months in prison for conducting cyberattacks against his former employer. The attacks involved deleting accounts and disrupting operations, causing tens of thousands of dollars in damages.

news.title: phpBB forum fixes auth bypass bug lurking for a decade
news.summary: phpBB forum software has patched a 10-year-old authentication bypass vulnerability that allowed attackers to log in as any user, including administrators. The flaw represents a complete authentication control failure, enabling full account takeover without valid credentials.

news.title: ShinyHunters Exploits Oracle PeopleSoft Zero-Day (CVE-2026-35273) to Breach Universities
news.summary: The ShinyHunters threat group exploited a zero-day vulnerability in Oracle PeopleSoft (CVE-2026-35273) to breach enterprise systems, primarily targeting universities, between May 27 and June 9. Oracle did not publish its advisory until June 10, meaning the exploitation occurred before a patch was available, with Mandiant attributing the activity to the group it tracks as UNC6240.

news.title: Linx Security Integrates with Claude Compliance API to Bring Identity Governance to Enterprise AI
news.summary: Linx Security announced an integration with Claude Compliance API to extend identity governance capabilities to enterprise AI environments. The integration targets hybrid workforce identity management, covering human, AI agent, and non-human identities.

news.title: Microsoft Entra adds device soft-delete and cloud-native sync migration
news.summary: Microsoft Entra has introduced a soft-delete feature for device objects in public preview, enabling administrators to recover accidentally deleted Entra joined or registered devices within a defined retention period. The update also includes cloud-native sync migration capabilities, preserving critical identity and device state data during recovery operations.

news.title: Levi & Korsinsky Announces Investigation of Securities Claims Against SailPoint, Inc. (SAIL)
news.summary: Law firm Levi & Korsinsky has announced a securities fraud investigation into SailPoint, Inc. (SAIL) following a significant stock drop of approximately 12% in a single session after a disappointing forward outlook disclosed on June 9, 2026. The investigation centers on whether SailPoint misled investors about its growth trajectory prior to the revised disclosure.

news.title: Teleport Named Most Innovative Identity and Access Management Platform in 2026 Cybersecurity Stars Awards
news.summary: Teleport has been named the most innovative Identity and Access Management Platform in The Hacker News' 2026 Cybersecurity Stars Awards. The recognition is specifically tied to Teleport's infrastructure identity architecture, which focuses on resiliency, scalability, and agility for modern computing environments.

news.title: IDMExpress Announces 24×7 Managed Services for Leading IAM/PAM Solutions with Innovative Unified Pricing Model
news.summary: IDMExpress announced a 24×7 Managed Identity Security Operations service covering IAM, PAM, and PKI platforms with a unified pricing model. The service offers a single bucket of hours usable across any supported platform with no vendor lock-in, available starting June 11, 2026.

news.title: Enterprise Identities Are Under Siege. Josys Is Changing How Organizations Fight Back.
news.summary: Josys announced new AI-native platform capabilities aimed at addressing enterprise identity security challenges including credential theft, AI agent governance, and SaaS access management. The announcement targets issues around identity governance and access policies in SaaS-first environments.

news.title: Not all AIs are equal so CIOs need to prioritize actions when assessing risks
news.summary: The article addresses how CIOs should approach identity governance and risk-based security controls as AI agents proliferate. The piece focuses on prioritizing actions when assessing risks across different AI systems in enterprise environments.

news.title: Threat actors are recruiting the people who hold cloud logins
news.summary: Threat actors are actively recruiting insiders who hold cloud platform credentials, targeting employees with access to organizational cloud logins. The cybercrime underground has developed systematic methods to exploit human access points rather than technical vulnerabilities, making credential-holding employees a primary attack vector.

news.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
news.summary: Hackers exploited Meta's AI support assistant bot to reset Instagram account passwords and seize accounts, including those of the Obama White House and a U.S. Space Force official. Instructions circulating on Telegram demonstrated how to social-engineer the AI bot into performing unauthorized password resets, bypassing normal account recovery authentication controls.

news.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
news.summary: A CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and other agency secrets to a public GitHub account, resulting in a data breach that CISA is still working to contain. Lawmakers from both chambers of Congress are demanding answers from CISA while the agency works to invalidate the leaked credentials.

news.title: CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
news.summary: A CISA contractor publicly exposed highly privileged AWS GovCloud credentials and internal system details in a public GitHub repository until this past weekend. The leaked repository contained files detailing CISA's internal software build, test, and deployment processes, representing a significant privileged credential exposure incident.
